data.title: Calidi Biotherapeutics prices $7.5M secondary offering
data.description:
• Calidi Biotherapeutics (NYSE:CLDI) announced secondary offering of ~6.94M shares

  • The offering comprises of 2.05M shares issuable on the exercise of Series E common warrants, exercisable at an exercise price of $1.13 per share, 2.05M shares of Common Stock issuable on the exercise of Series F common warrants, exercisable at an exercise price of $1.13 per share, and 221,893 shares of Common Stock issued to Ladenburg

  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of securities.
  • The closing of the offering took place on November 15, 2024.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering were approximately $7.5M.

data.title: Lovesac sinks after issuing soft guidance due to category headwinds
data.description:
The Lovesac Company (NASDAQ:LOVE) fell sharply in early trading on Thursday after reporting a 2.7% year-over-year drop in revenue during Q3 to miss the consensus estimate by $5.4 million. The decline during the quarter was driven by a decrease of 8.3% in omnichannel comparable sales, partially offset by the addition of 28 new showrooms.

Gross margin increased 110 basis points to 58.5% of sales in the quarter, primarily driven by decreases of 120 basis points in inbound transportation costs and 40 basis points in outbound transportation and warehousing costs, partially offset by a decrease of 50 basis points in product margin driven by higher promotional discounting. EPS was reported at -$0.32 vs. -$0.35 consensus and -$0.15 a year ago.

CEO Shawn Nelson said despite near-term headwinds for the category clearly that persisted through the pre-election period, the company gained market share and strengthened its competitive position through its focus on product innovation and operational excellence. Nelson said the soft launch of its reclining seat in the quarter is just one of many exciting examples on the way.

Looking ahead, Lovesac (LOVE) sees Q4 revenue of $221 million to $241 million vs. $269 million and EPS of $1.67 to $2.14 vs. $2.53 consensus.

Shares of Lovesac Company (LOVE) nosedived 22.1% in the first 15 minutes of trading on Thursday. The company has an investor event schedule for next week.

data.title: Terreno Realty acquires property in Brooklyn, New York, for ~$156.3M
data.description:
• Industrial REIT Terreno Realty (NYSE:TRNO) acquired a property in Red Hook, Brooklyn, New York, on December 11 for ~$156.3M.
  • The purchase price includes the assumption of ~$72.9M 3.85% interest-only loan, maturing in March 2028.
  • The property consists of one 36-foot clear height industrial distribution building containing ~312K leasable sq. ft. on 16 acres.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Qualcomm appoints Baaziz Achour to the role of CTO
data.description:
In a key leadership transition, Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) on Thursday appointed Baaziz Achour to the role of Chief Technology Officer, effective February 3, 2025.

Achour, who currently serves as deputy Chief Technology Officer, will succeed James Thompson, the company said. Thompson is set to retire as CTO after 33 years at the company.

Achour has first joined Qualcomm as a Systems Engineer in 1993 and has held several leadership roles within the engineering organization over the course of his tenure. According to the company, he played an important role in the accelerated launch of 5G and is also expected to lead the company's evolution to 6G.

“During his time as CTO, Jim not only advanced Qualcomm’s wireless technology leadership but also expanded our capabilities, including in advanced processors and AI,” said Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon.

Shares of the California-based chipmaker were down over 1% in morning trading on Thursday. Overall, the stock has gained 9% so far this year, compared to the nearly 28% rise in the broader S&P500 Index.

data.title: Wall Street wavers after producer inflation data comes in hot
data.description:
U.S. stocks were largely lower on Thursday after the opening bell, following some key producer inflation data that came in hotter than expected.

Minutes after market open, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.20%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.36%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.02%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 2 basis points to 4.29%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 1 basis point to 4.17%.

Before the start of regular trading, the November Producer Price Index (PPI) came in at +0.4% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus, and +3.0% Y/Y vs. +2.6%. Core PPI came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus, and +3.4% Y/Y vs. +3.2% consensus.

Separately, initial jobless claims unexpectedly jumped in the past week.

U.S. stocks finished mostly higher in Wednesday's history-making session, as an in-line consumer inflation report bolstered expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week. Nasdaq had ended above 20K, with the Big Tech boosting the index.

"Markets put in a decent performance yesterday, as the U.S. CPI report came in broadly as expected, which was seen as giving the all-clear for the Federal Reserve to cut rates next week," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"When it comes to the Fed, the CPI print left little doubt among investors that another rate cut will happen next week," Allen added.

Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 10.2% after the Photoshop creator issued guidance for fiscal 2025 that fell short of Wall Street's expectations.

More on markets

  • Behind the monster rally that drove the Nasdaq to 20,000
  • CPI Report: Inflation Is No Longer A Concern
  • Reaction Roundup: Experts weigh in on November consumer inflation report
  • The Recipe For A Bull Market

data.title: Riot Platforms jumps after report activist Starboard takes stake
data.description: Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) soared 10% in premarket trading after a report that activist Starboard Value has taken a stake in the bitcoin miner.

data.title: Novo Nordisk gets EMA nod for potential update of Ozempic label
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk (NVO) announced that the European Medicines Agency’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use has adopted a positive opinion for an update of the Ozempic label to reflect data from the FLOW kidney outcomes trial.
  • The FLOW trial suggested that semaglutide 1.0 mg demonstrated a statistically significant and superior 24% risk reduction in kidney disease progression as well as cardiovascular and kidney death compared to placebo.

data.title: MicroStrategy stands to gain from potential Nasdaq 100 inclusion: Bernstein
data.description:
MicroStrategy's (NASDAQ:MSTR) growth engine could shift into overdrive, Bernstein suggested in a recent note, as the company's potential inclusion in the Nasdaq 100 Index might amplify its bitcoin-driven momentum and attract more capital for further bitcoin (BTC-USD) purchases.

Shares of the self-proclaimed bitcoin (BTC-USD) development company co-founded by Michael Saylor have already surged some 550% this year, as it intensified its bold strategy to raise capital exclusively for buying and holding bitcoin, the price of which has also been on a tear.

"With potential inclusion in Nasdaq100 and QQQ, we expect more visibility and recognition beyond fresh ETF inflows," analyst Gautam Chhugani, who has an Outperform rating on MSTR, wrote in a note to clients.

"And if the Nasdaq100 inclusion happens, the market will likelyset its sight on S&P 500 inclusion for 2025," though that may be a challenge "due to profitability of its software business."

Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas noted that MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) will likely be added to the Nasdaq 100 on Dec. 23 with a 0.46% weight in the index, according to a recent post on X.

In all, the MicroStrategy (MSTR) "flywheel is going to further accelerate from here," Chhugani contended, as the U.S. set to ratchet up its focus on crypto regulation after President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.

Bernstein's Outperform rate diverges from the SA Quant system rating of Hold and the average SA analyst of Sell, but aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Strong Buy.

(MSTR) shares edged up 0.5% in premarket trading.

data.title: GoGold Resources GAAP EPS of $0.002, revenue of $10.41M
data.description:
• GoGold Resources press release (OTCQX:GLGDF): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.002.
  • Revenue of $10.41M (+83.0% Y/Y).
  • Highlights for the year ending September 30, 2024: With Parral revenue increasing by 20%, generating $37 million from the sale of 1.4 million silver equivalent ounces.
  • Production of 1,482,391 silver equivalent ounces, consisting of 553,382 silver ounces, 8,700 gold ounces, 468 copper tonnes, 316 zinc tonnes.
  • Adjusted cash cost per silver equivalent ounce of $17.62.
  • Adjusted all in sustaining cost per silver equivalent ounce of $24.15.

data.title: Crown Laboratories commences tender offer for Revance Therapeutics at $3.10 per share
data.description:
• Crown Laboratories commenced a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Revance Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RVNC) at a price of $3.10 per share in cash.
  • The Revance (RVNC) Board believed the certainty of value provided by the $3.10 cash offer was in the best interest of stockholders.
  • The tender offer is being made in connection with the amended and restated merger agreement, and will expire on January 13, 2025, unless extended.
  • The stock price increased 2% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.
